sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Tensions and Tech Earnings Weigh on Futures

items.headline: U.S. Futures Decline as Middle East Tensions Escalate and Key Tech Earnings Disappoint
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are trending lower this morning as investors react to rising oil prices stemming from escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and mixed signals from recent technology earnings reports. Concerns over increased AI-related capital expenditures from companies like Alphabet are dampening sentiment. The broader market sentiment, as indicated by the CNN Money Fear and Greed index, remained in the “Fear” zone on Wednesday.

items.headline: Hyliion Surges on U.S. Navy Contract Win
items.summary: Shares of Hyliion surged 15% in premarket trading after the company secured a $41.7 million U.S. Navy contract for KARNO power generation systems.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Jumps on New Orders
items.summary: Super Micro Computer saw its shares climb 19.8%, driven by over $60 billion in new orders.

items.headline: Lockheed Martin Rises on Raised Forecasts and Strong Q2 Earnings
items.summary: Lockheed Martin rose 5.3% in premarket trading after reporting strong second-quarter results, with sales of $20.1 billion and net earnings of $7.94 per share, and subsequently lifting its 2026 sales and profit forecasts.

items.headline: Nasdaq Inc. Beats Q2 Expectations, Stock Up Premarket
items.summary: Nasdaq Inc. experienced a pre-market rise of 2.31% after its second-quarter earnings surpassed analyst forecasts, reporting a non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 and net revenue of $1.50 billion.

items.headline: Alphabet Shares Decline on Increased Capex Guidance and Regulatory Fine
items.summary: Alphabet shares are falling, extending declines from Wednesday, after the company raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to as much as $205 billion. Additionally, European regulators fined the tech giant 890 million euros ($1 billion).

items.headline: Tesla Slips After Missing Profit Estimates and Higher Capex Plans
items.summary: Tesla shares are lower, following its Q2 earnings report on Wednesday, where the company missed profit estimates and outlined plans for higher capital spending.

items.headline: GE Vernova Falls on Disappointing Outlook
items.summary: GE Vernova shares fell 8.7% in early trading following a disappointing outlook.

sections.title: Market News: Key Economic and Sector Developments

items.headline: Utilities Lead Gains Amid AI Data Center Boom
items.summary: The Utilities sector was the top-performing sector on Wednesday, gaining 2.28%, driven by a recovery in AI stocks and positive sentiment towards power companies benefiting from the data center boom.

items.headline: Communication Services Lagging, Technology Under Pressure
items.summary: The Communication Services sector was the worst-performing sector on Wednesday, losing 1.29%. Technology stocks broadly came under pressure, with the Nasdaq declining, as investors reduced exposure to higher-growth sectors amid rising bond yields.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Amid Inflationary Pressures
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is anticipated to maintain its policy interest rates steady in the 3.50%-3.75% range at next week's FOMC meeting (July 28-29). However, rising oil prices and potential tariffs may challenge Chairman Kevin Warsh's ability to build consensus for this stance.

items.headline: Intel, Honeywell, and American Airlines Among Companies Reporting Earnings Today
items.summary: Investors are closely watching for earnings reports today from major companies including Intel ($INTC), Honeywell International Inc. ($HON), and American Airlines ($AAL). Lockheed Martin ($LMT) and First Citizens BancShares ($FCNCA) have already reported strong Q2 results, while Nasdaq ($NDAQ) also beat expectations.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge on Heightened Middle East Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices are climbing significantly today, with Brent crude up 3.26% to $95.60 a barrel and WTI crude rising 4.31% to $90.65 a barrel. This surge is attributed to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, particularly the U.S.-Iran conflict.

items.headline: Buy Now, Pay Later Adoption Nears Record High Amid Financial Strain
items.summary: New data reveals that overall 'Buy Now, Pay Later' (BNPL) adoption is nearing a record high, with 15% of active cardholders using such services in Q2. This trend is particularly pronounced among lower-income households earning under $40,000 annually and families with children, indicating deepening financial strain.
